_doc_: Template scoring model for tools/score.py — copy this to tmp/scoring-model.json,
edit the rules + tier labels for your ICP, then run:
Shape conventions (validated 2026-05-05 on the canonical 50-row demo):
- 'tiers' is for label/sales_motion lookup; the 'tier' integer in 'rules' is the source of truth
- 'rules' evaluated top-to-bottom; FIRST MATCH wins (no override scoring)
- Put hard disqualifiers FIRST so they short-circuit before positive tiers fire
- Put most-specific positive tiers (Tier 1) before looser ones (Tier 2, 3) for proper
rank ordering — a Tier-1-eligible row must hit the Tier 1 rule before falling through
- 'default_tier' is the fallback when no rule matches; usually the worst non-disqualifier tier
eq, neq, in, not_in, gt, gte, lt, lte, between (value=[lo,hi]), is_null, not_null
Numeric ops treat blank/null as 'not matching' — null employee_count safely falls through.
When rules need fuzzy/judgment ('this looks like a Series B Fintech') use
tools/score.py is for crisp deterministic rules only.
name: saas-tier-rubric-template
description: Replace this with a one-line description of your ICP's scoring rubric.

tiers.label: Ideal
tiers.sales_motion: [fill in: e.g. Multi-touch outbound + AE assignment within 24h]

rules._note: Disqualifier evaluated FIRST so non-US SaaS doesn't accidentally land Tier 1
Most-specific positive rule fires before Tier 2's looser rule
Looser positive — covers 11-49 emp AND 2001+ emp (since both >10 but outside Tier 1 band)
Falls through here when SaaS+US but emp is null or <=10. Could also use is_null + lt and combine with match_any.

notes: country='US' check excludes null country (treats unknown HQ as disqualified). If you want
null country routed to manual review instead of disqualified, add a separate rule for
{field: country, op: is_null} -> tier 3 BEFORE the Tier 4 disqualifier.
